why: The owner explicitly rejects blind handling of opening `و`; it may simply continue the same subject locally.
risk_if_handled_blindly: A rigid protocol would either import unnecessary context or strip a harmless local continuation incorrectly.
preferred_handling_in_this_case: Diagnose intelligently; do not let the opening `و` decide the verdict by itself.

why: The tarjih marker depends on the already mentioned existence of multiple views inside the same excerptal surface.
risk_if_handled_blindly: If split away carelessly, it becomes an orphaned preference marker without a stated field of comparison.
preferred_handling_in_this_case: If the tarjih is split out, complete it with its preferred-view statement in the engineering layer.

why: As given, the excerpt does not state what the الجمهور view actually is; the surrounding context provides the completion.
risk_if_handled_blindly: The unit may be falsely accepted as a complete tarjih even though the preferred view is unfinished.
preferred_handling_in_this_case: Use surrounding context diagnostically to complete the tarjih candidate in the engineering layer only.

why: The as-given excerpt is not just short; one of its two functions needs surrounding context to complete.
risk_if_handled_blindly: The owner verdict could be softened incorrectly by silently using surrounding context to repair the excerpt.
preferred_handling_in_this_case: Keep the owner verdict on the excerpt as given negative, and restrict surrounding-context use to engineering reconstruction only.
